strengths: Clearly identifies three distinct real-world objects and provides recognizable contexts for each
Explicitly references the IA prompt for each object, anchoring claims in concrete details
Insightful connections drawn between specialist notation and imaginative knowledge construction
Consistent labelling and organisation aid clarity and navigability
Repeated quotes and paraphrases of the prompt demonstrate deliberate engagement
weaknesses: Explanations of links to the prompt are uneven, drifting into descriptive narrative or anecdote
Key TOK concepts are invoked without precise definition or sustained critical development
Supporting evidence is often asserted rather than rigorously substantiated or cited
Justifications across objects remain partial and at times repetitive, lacking integrated synthesis
Citation formatting is inconsistent and historical or theoretical claims are frequently unreferenced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examiner award of 6 reflects a 5â6 band performance: the student goes beyond superficial description by linking each object to the IA prompt and supplying some evidence, but does not achieve the clarity, precision and comprehensive support required for higher bands. Strengths include clear object identification, deliberate prompt referencing and insightful specialist-notation analysis. However, the analysis is uneven in depth, relies on unsupported assertions, and fails to synthesise insights across all objects. On balance, the work merits 6 out of 10, recognising its clear engagement with the prompt and concrete details, while acknowledging its uneven TOK development and documentation.

annotations.comment: The student clearly presents the IA prompt in italics, ensuring the exhibitionâs focus is immediately apparent.
The introduction lacks an overview of how the chosen objects will collectively address the prompt; consider adding a brief rationale linking all three objects.
The section clearly labels the first object, aiding navigability and contextual clarity.
The analysis does not explicitly tie the distorted reflection image to core sense-perception limitations and imaginative reconstruction; articulate this link more clearly.
This paragraph is largely descriptive; deepen the TOK analysis by explaining how imagination actively supplements incomplete sense data in knowledge production.
The student insightfully connects cultural language influences and technological advances to how imagination shapes scientific models, showing emerging TOK understanding.
While the cover art description is detailed, the student should analyze visual elements to explain how they metaphorically represent imaginative processes in knowledge creation.
Historical claim regarding Maria Anna Mozart is unverified; include a scholarly reference to substantiate the biographical context.
The historical claim about 2000 BCE bronze mirrors is asserted but not critically examined; include specific evidence or scholarly insight to support this context.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent; adopt a standard citation style and include complete bibliographic details for academic rigor.
Contextualising the object within CAS demonstrates a clear real-world application, strengthening the justification of its inclusion.
The link to âspecialist notationâ from the IA prompt is implied but not explicitly referenced; quote or paraphrase the prompt to clarify this connection.
Generalisations about beauty standards across cultures lack citations and TOK theory; define âcommunities of knowersâ and provide references to support these cultural contrasts.
The analogy to distant planets is too broad; develop this example to show how cultural values and imagination concretely affect hypothesis formation in science.
This narrative is descriptive; foreground TOK concepts like shared knowledge and imaginative perspective-taking to deepen the theoretical analysis.
Analysis of fantastical versus realistic language mentions imagination but omits discussion of how linguistic structures shape knowledge claims; elaborate on this relationship.
The statement âimagination makes experiences more relatableâ lacks critical depth; examine implications for the credibility and objectivity of knowledge produced.
The discussion of imagination as communication is promising but underdeveloped; include concrete examples of miscommunication affecting knowledge generation.
The final argument does not synthesise insights across all three objects; add a conclusive reflection to demonstrate the exhibitionâs integrated coherence.
Describing the pattern abbreviations as âgibberishâ is dismissive; analyze how these symbols function as a formal language within the crocheting community of knowers.
The insight that expertise enables imaginative recombination of core knowledge reflects a meaningful engagement with how imagination contributes to knowledge creation.
The claim âonly things that are possible can be imaginedâ is philosophically debatable; clarify this assertion and relate it to how scientific imagination expands conceptual boundaries.
The lightbulb example is historically interesting but unreferenced; link this case to specific processes of model-based reasoning in knowledge production with proper citation.
The transition from crochet to scientific discovery feels abrupt; provide a clearer justification for cross-disciplinary analogy to maintain coherence.
The personal anecdote about the book exchange enriches understanding of how imagination plays a role in interpersonal knowledge sharing.
The title of this object does not indicate why the novel was chosen; consider adding a brief explanation of its relevance to the prompt in the header.
Across the commentary, key TOK terms are used without definition; consider defining terms like âimaginationâ, âknowledgeâ, and âcommunities of knowersâ to strengthen clarity.
The concept of âcommunities of knowersâ is invoked but not explained; define this term and illustrate how community dynamics influence imaginative processes.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
